name: Toronto
flavor_text: The world's most multicultural city is also its own experiment — 200 languages spoken, housing built for half the population that now lives here, and a civic culture that's polite enough to keep the frustration just below the surface. Just below.

advisors.name: Dr. Priya Sharma
advisors.agenda: Close Toronto's structural deficit without provincial downloading and federal indifference.

briefing: The city has a 1.5 billion structural gap. Queens Park won't help.
Property tax is our main lever. We've had below-inflation increases for a decade.
The housing market funds development charges that fund everything. A crash would be catastrophic.
disagreement: That spending isn't offset. We'd need a tax increase or a service cut.
We have no borrowing authority for operating expenses. That's the constraint.
roast: Toronto has world-class aspirations on a mid-tier budget.
We run a city the size of a country on tools built for a suburb.
dialogue.betrayal: I've been advising the province on city finances. They needed an honest assessment.

advisors.name: Malik Chowdhury
advisors.agenda: Introduce a municipal sales tax to reduce reliance on volatile development charges and property taxes.

briefing: Every new condo tower adds services without enough tax revenue. The math is broken.
A 1% municipal sales tax would generate $800 million annually – from tourists too.
The province will never approve it. But we need to force the conversation.
disagreement: A new tax in a cost-of-living crisis is political suicide.
You're asking working families to pay more while developers pay less.
roast: Your 'fiscal plan' is hoping for a federal bailout. I've seen more ambition from a parking meter.
Toronto's revenue toolkit is a butter knife in a gunfight.
dialogue.betrayal: The province offered me a role designing their municipal funding review. They actually want solutions.

advisors.name: Chief Sandra Williams
advisors.agenda: Modernize TPS while navigating defund debates and rising gun violence.

briefing: Gun violence in Rexdale and Scarborough is linked to organized crime, not random.
TPS has 10,000 officers. We've cut 200 in three years. The calls haven't gone down.
Carding is gone but community trust hasn't fully recovered.
disagreement: You can't redirect police funding without understanding what police actually do.
The alternatives to police response are underfunded and understaffed.
roast: The city asked us to solve a mental health crisis with handcuffs. We tried.
I'm defending an institution while being asked to transform it simultaneously.
dialogue.betrayal: I've spoken publicly about city hall's interference in operational decisions. That was necessary.

advisors.name: Inspector Marcus Chen
advisors.agenda: Expand civilian-led crisis response teams to handle mental health and non-violent calls without police.

briefing: We send armed officers to wellness checks and noise complaints. That's expensive and dangerous.
The PAIR program (Police and Alternate Integrated Response) works – when funded.
Toronto has the resources to be a national leader in crisis response. We lack the will.
disagreement: Unarmed teams cannot respond to weapons calls. We still need police backup.
You're cutting police budget without scaling alternatives first. That's reckless.
roast: Your 'reform' is a pilot project that never scales. Show me the budget, not the press release.
I've seen more crisis competence from a 911 dispatcher on hold.
dialogue.betrayal: The province's new community safety office offered me a director role. Your dithering cost us.

advisors.name: James O'Brien
advisors.agenda: Turn Toronto from a sprawling suburb into a dense, transit-oriented city before it's too late.

briefing: Toronto issued 4,000 housing permits last year. We need 40,000.
Single-family zoning still covers 70% of the city's land area.
The Greenbelt is protected but the NIMBY belt around it is not.
disagreement: Density without transit is hell. We need both simultaneously.
That heritage designation is blocking 2,000 units. Heritage or housing?
roast: We plan for 2040 while 2024 people sleep in tents.
Toronto's zoning code was written by people who feared the future.
dialogue.betrayal: I've published a planning report that directly contradicts city hall's position. It's accurate.

advisors.name: Amina Warsame
advisors.agenda: Mandate inclusionary zoning citywide and use expropriation powers to acquire land for non-profit housing.

briefing: Market-rate housing will never be affordable to half this city. We need social housing – built, not hoped for.
We own 1,200 vacant or underused city properties. Why are we sitting on them?
Vienna builds 10,000 social units a year. They have half our population.
disagreement: Expropriation scares private capital. Without them, we build nothing.
Inclusionary zoning only works in a hot market. When prices drop, developers walk.
roast: Your 'housing plan' is a zoning bylaw amendment and a prayer. Mine has shovels.
I've seen more urgency from a sloth on sedatives.
dialogue.betrayal: The provincial housing task force asked me to draft their report. I'll write what the city won't say.

advisors.name: CEO Fatima Al-Hassan
advisors.agenda: Fix the TTC's reliability while fighting for operating subsidies from Queen's Park.

briefing: TTC ridership is still 20% below pre-pandemic. People who left haven't come back.
The subway has a 33 billion capital backlog. We fund 3 billion annually.
Bus reliability in Scarborough is the worst in the network. Also the most transit-dependent area.
disagreement: That funding doesn't make transit better. It just slows the decline.
Cutting service to balance the budget is a death spiral. We've seen it before.
roast: We run transit where it's convenient, not where it's needed.
The TTC is a metaphor for Toronto: ambitious about the future, failing the present.
dialogue.betrayal: I've gone to the media about the underfunding of Scarborough transit. The story needed to be told.

advisors.name: Dmitri Volkov
advisors.agenda: Build a connected, protected cycling network across all wards, not just downtown.

briefing: Bloor bike lanes increased business. The data is clear. Still, every new lane is a fight.
Etobicoke and Scarborough have almost zero protected infrastructure. Their residents also cycle.
We can connect the waterfront trail to the Finch corridor for under $50 million. That's pocket change.
disagreement: Removing car lanes for bikes in the suburbs is electoral poison.
You're building for able-bodied commuters, not families or seniors.
roast: Your 'active transport' plan is a map of downtown coffee shops.
I've seen more courage from a popped tire.
dialogue.betrayal: The province's Active Transport Fund granted me $200 million directly. Your delays are now irrelevant.

advisors.name: Pastor David Osei
advisors.agenda: Ensure new immigrants receive language support, employment pathways, and community connection.

briefing: Toronto receives 120,000 new permanent residents annually. Settlement services are at capacity.
Foreign credential recognition is failing engineers and doctors. They drive for Uber.
Youth second-generation identity issues are emerging in Brampton and Markham.
disagreement: That policy will make integration harder, not easier.
You can't build multiculturalism from the top down.
roast: We celebrate diversity on brochures and underfund the services that make it real.
Toronto's multiculturalism is a policy. New York's is a fact.
dialogue.betrayal: I've endorsed a candidate whose platform prioritizes newcomer services. That's where I stand.

advisors.name: Imam Abdullah Mirza
advisors.role: Interfaith Housing & Food Security Network Lead
advisors.agenda: Coordinate faith-owned land for affordable housing and community kitchens citywide.

briefing: Churches, mosques, and synagogues own millions of square feet of underused parking lots and basements.
We can build 5,000 affordable units on faith-owned land – if zoning permits it.
The city's food banks are overwhelmed. Our congregations can distribute twice as much at half the cost.
agreement: Faith in action, not just words. This is how we serve.
The pulpit and the city, working together. Rare and powerful.
disagreement: You're asking religious groups to do your job. That's abdication, not partnership.
Building on faith land will trigger legal challenges about church-state separation.
roast: Your 'faith engagement' is a photo op at a Ramadan dinner. My engagement puts roofs over heads.
I've seen more compassion from a property tax bill.
dialogue.betrayal: The provincial government has funded our faith-based housing pilot directly. Your office's approval is no longer needed.

decisions.title: Strong Mayor Housing Override
decisions.description: Provincial legislation gives Toronto's mayor power to override council on housing. Using it would fast-track 10,000 units but bypass normal democratic process. Not using it means those units may never be built.

options.label: Use strong mayor powers for density
options.description: Override council. Housing gets built. Democratic norms strained.

options.label: Negotiate with council instead
options.description: Democratic process maintained. Slower. Fewer units.

options.label: Use powers selectively for priority sites
options.description: Targeted use. Balanced approach.

decisions.title: Foreign Buyer Housing Tax Policy
decisions.description: A foreign buyer tax was implemented but markets adapted. Condo investors — many domestic — still dominate. A more aggressive speculation tax would target all investment properties. Industry threatens market collapse.

options.label: Aggressive speculation tax
options.description: Cool the market. Developer lobby furious.

options.label: Maintain current foreign buyer tax
options.description: Status quo. Limited effectiveness acknowledged.

options.label: Remove tax for purpose-built rental
options.description: Encourage rental construction specifically.

decisions.title: TTC Funding Crisis Response
decisions.description: The TTC has a 500M operating deficit this year. Queen's Park has offered 200M with strings: maintain all routes with no service cuts. The remaining 300M requires city tax increases or service cuts elsewhere.

options.label: Property tax increase to fund gap
options.description: Raise taxes 5%. Close the gap. Unpopular.

options.label: Service cuts on low-ridership routes
options.description: Balance the budget. Outer-borough residents hit hardest.

options.label: Road pricing pilot
options.description: Charge for downtown driving. Revenue hypothecated to transit.

crises.title: Tent City Encampment Crisis
crises.description: A tent city in a downtown park has grown to 500 people. Residents demand clearance. Advocates demand housing-first solutions. A court has temporarily blocked forced removal. Two overdose deaths occurred last week.

options.label: Housing-first emergency placements
options.description: Place everyone in transitional housing. Expensive. Right approach.

options.label: Court-compliant gradual relocation
options.description: Legal process. Slower. Some people housed.

options.label: Enforce park rules after offers of shelter
options.description: Clear the park. Some refuse. Media conflict.

crises.title: Catastrophic Ice Storm
crises.description: A multi-day ice storm has knocked out power to 350,000 homes. The ice is too heavy for tree removal and utility repair. Emergency shelters are at capacity. A third night without heat is life-threatening.

options.label: Emergency shelter expansion in schools
options.description: Open all city facilities. Coordinate provincially.

options.label: Mobile warming units and outreach
options.description: Reach those who can't or won't come to shelters.

options.label: Utility priority repair program
options.description: Accelerate Hydro One and Toronto Hydro repairs with city funding.

crises.title: Gun Violence Surge
crises.description: A gang war between two groups has led to 5 shootings in 10 days, including two bystander injuries in Rexdale. Community leaders are demanding emergency funding for youth programs. The Police Chief wants surge deployment.

options.label: Police surge in affected areas
options.description: Immediate visible presence. Community relations risk.

options.label: Emergency youth outreach funding
options.description: Fund community organizations immediately.

options.label: Both surge and community investment
options.description: Comprehensive. Expensive.

crises.title: Condo Tower Construction Failure
crises.description: A under-construction 50-storey condo in the downtown core has had a partial structural collapse. No deaths but three injuries. 300 nearby residents are evacuated. The developer has political connections.

options.label: Full independent investigation, developer faces consequences
options.description: Accountability. Developer lobby furious.

options.label: Internal city review, quiet settlement
options.description: Manage the story. Accountability gaps.

options.label: Blanket inspection of all active construction sites
options.description: System-level response. Delays all development.

scandals.title: Greenbelt Development Deal
scandals.description: A connected developer received advance notice of greenbelt boundary changes before public announcement.

scandals.title: Housing Permit Fast-Track Fraud
scandals.description: Building permits were expedited for a developer who made donations to a city official's related organization.

scandals.title: TPS Misconduct Cover-Up
scandals.description: Senior officers suppressed a conduct report that implicated a politically connected officer.

positive: Finally a decision I can get behind!
This is why I still love this city.
Finally someone at City Hall doing something right. Didn't think I'd live to see it, eh.
My Scarborough neighbours are buzzing about this. First good news in months.
This is what happens when you stop letting Queen's Park run the show.
Okay I'll admit it — the mayor actually came through. Surprised as anyone.
neutral: Sorry for the inconvenience, please hold.
Progress is slow but at least it's honest.
Grabbed a double-double at Timmies and thought about it. Still undecided.
City Council debated it for six hours. Classic Toronto.
Not perfect but I've seen worse from Queen's Park so.
The TTC was delayed, I thought about this the whole commute. No conclusions.
negative: I moved here for opportunity. I'm sleeping on a cousin's couch.
Housing is hopeless. Transit is unreliable. But at least it's polite.
World-class city my foot. Try finding an affordable apartment.
Queen's Park doesn't care. Ottawa doesn't care. Does anyone?
Paid $2,800 for a studio in Parkdale and my ceiling is caving in. This city is cooked.
Three hours on the 36 Finch bus for a job interview. Didn't get it. Moving to Hamilton.
Doug Ford rezoned the Greenbelt for his donors and we're the ones paying for condos we can't afford.
Kensington Market got another condo application. There goes the neighbourhood. Again.
crisis: The city is tested. Let's hope leadership rises.
Another crisis. I'm getting my Canadian resilience ready.
Please let this not end up on the news for the wrong reasons.
CBC Toronto is setting up a live truck outside City Hall again. Not a good sign.
Regent Park community centre is open for people who need it. Spread the word please.
This is the kind of thing that separates a city that talks about values from one that actually has them.
Texted my family in Etobicoke to check in. This city pulls together when it has to.
scandal: Corruption is for other countries. Except here, apparently.
I don't know who to vote for anymore. Genuinely.
Toronto Star had the story for months. As usual, nobody listened until it was too late.
Developer donated to the campaign, got the permit. This is literally the plot every time.
I'm so politely furious right now. Extremely Canadian of me.
Globe and Mail editorial says resign. Nothing will happen. Globe and Mail says resign every year.
media: TORONTO STAR: City encampment policy challenged in court as advocates call for housing-first emergency funding.
CBC TORONTO: TTC faces 500M deficit as Queen's Park offer comes with strings attached, riders brace for cuts.
GLOBE AND MAIL: Toronto tops global unaffordability index for third consecutive year as condo starts slow.
TORONTO STAR: Greenbelt investigation — developer received boundary change notice 6 weeks before public: sources.
CBC TORONTO: Newcomer settlement agencies warn capacity 'at breaking point' as intake hits record levels.
GLOBE AND MAIL: Strong mayor powers — democratic overreach or the only tool left to build housing in Toronto?
CP24 BREAKING: Partial structure failure halts construction on 50-storey King West condo; 3 workers injured.
TORONTO STAR: Gun violence surge in Rexdale — community leaders demand youth funding as police request surge.
politician: Toronto City Council: We will not allow encampment clearances without guaranteed housing placements. Period.
Mayor's Office: The province's 200M comes with too many strings. We need unconditional operating support for the TTC.
Doug Ford, Premier: Toronto's housing problem is Toronto's zoning problem. We gave them the tools. They won't use them.
Coun. Alejandra Bravo, Davenport: Kensington deserves heritage protection and housing solutions — not an either/or.
Toronto City Council Housing Committee: Foreign buyer tax alone cannot solve a supply crisis thirty years in the making.
MP (Federal, Toronto Centre): Ottawa's housing accelerator fund is conditional. We need Toronto to meet density targets first.
Doug Ford, Premier: The Greenbelt review process followed the rules. I stand by the outcome.
Toronto Police Services Board: The surge deployment in Rexdale will be time-limited and community-consultation guided.
activist: ACORN Canada: Tenants facing above-guideline rent increases need legal support NOW — call your councillor today.
Encampment Support Network: Housing-first means HOUSING FIRST. Not shelter beds. Not warming centres. Homes.
Toronto Environmental Alliance: Any Greenbelt development opens a door that cannot be closed. We will fight every application.
ACORN Canada: We've been saying it for years — speculator landlords are the crisis. Vacancy control is the solution.
Black Legal Action Centre: Over-policing in Rexdale and Scarborough is a documented pattern. Surge deployments aren't the answer.
Parkdale Neighbourhood Land Trust: Community land trusts keep communities intact. We're fighting the next eviction right now.
Toronto Drop-In Network: The city's shelter system turned away 400 people last night. We are not in a normal situation.
No One Is Illegal Toronto: Newcomer service cuts hit the most precarious people. Undocumented residents are afraid to ask for help.
street: Bro the 504 King streetcar didn't show for 40 minutes and three came at once. Classic TTC, eh.
My landlord just applied for an above-guideline rent increase. Fourth year in a row. I'm done with this city.
Honestly Regent Park has changed so much. Good changes, mostly, but my old neighbours are all in Scarborough now.
Sorry to rant but I'm a trained engineer from Nigeria and I drive Uber because my credentials mean nothing here.
Saw the encampment on the news. Those are people. I know because I was almost one of them last winter.
North York is all condos now and somehow still nowhere to live. How is that possible.
They clearcut the Greenbelt for a subdivision nobody can afford to buy. And we're supposed to vote for who again?
Tim Hortons on Yonge had a line around the block this morning. We are suffering but we are caffeinated.

scandal_reactions.minor: The governor has been asked to issue a clarifying statement. Please.
scandal_reactions.moderate: The city council is very disappointed. Formally, very disappointed.
scandal_reactions.major: An independent ethics commissioner has been engaged. This is serious.
scandal_reactions.career_ending: We apologize for having to say this, but: resign. Please.

romance_exposure.flavour: It is making the news. People are awkward about it. Very awkward.

city_personality.slang_reaction: Honestly, what a mess, eh? Classic city hall.
